adventures-of-brave-bob.title: Adventures of Brave Bob
adventures-of-brave-bob.description:
Sir Bob is the humblest knight in the realm, but when a giant lilac dragon kidnaps the princess he refuses to sit on the bench. To survive the dragon's lair he must first battle through waves of minions, earning coins and experience with every fight.

A wise scarecrow mentor takes Bob under his wing — teaching defence, timing, and the art of dodging flying apples. Spend your coins on shiny new armour, sharpen your sword, and piece together a warrior capable of facing the final boss. The dragon won't know what hit it.
intro: A knight, a dragon, and a princess who needs rescuing — your classic adventure begins.
Train hard, upgrade your gear, and prove Bob is more than a lowly squire.
adventures-of-brave-bob.controls: Move Bob

baseball.title: Doodle Baseball
baseball.description:
Created for the Fourth of July 2011, Doodle Baseball replaces nine innings of tradition with pure arcade fun. A mischievous peanut is on the mound, a dancing hot dog cheers from the dugout, and you are the batter. The controls are one button — everything else is timing.

Watch the pitch arc, swing at the sweet spot, and keep your hit streak alive as the crowd goes wild. Miss three times and the inning is over, so concentrate, read the delivery, and see how many runs you can rack up before the fireworks start.
intro: Fast-food mascots step up to the plate in Google's Independence Day Doodle.
Swing at the perfect moment to send hot dogs and peanuts flying.
baseball.controls: Swing the bat or or

basket-random-pro.title: Basket Random Pro
basket-random-pro.description:
Basket Random Pro throws out every coaching manual ever written. Your two-player team stumbles, moonwalks, and helicopters toward the hoop under a physics engine that treats gravity as a polite suggestion. Scoring a basket feels like an achievement worthy of a trophy.

Each round randomly changes the court, the characters, and the forces at play, so no two games look the same. Forget strategy — just mash your key at the right moment and hope the ball somehow arcs into the net. First to seven points wins, loser demands a rematch.
intro: The most chaotic basketball game ever made — and completely unplayable in the best way.
Each round scrambles the rules; the first to seven wins.
basket-random-pro.controls:
Player 1
Player 1 — jump and shoot

basketball-2012-google-doodle.title: Basketball 2012 Google Doodle
basketball-2012-google-doodle.description:
Google built this Doodle to celebrate basketball at the 2012 London Olympics. The challenge is simple: sink as many baskets as possible before the buzzer sounds. The ball's position relative to the hoop shifts after every shot, so you can't just repeat the same motion.

Press Space or click your mouse to release the ball on a timed arc. Get into a rhythm, read the angle, and chain together a scoring streak. The crowd roars louder with every successful basket — and goes very quiet when you miss an easy one.
intro: The 2012 London Olympics inspired this one-button basketball Doodle.
Click or press Space to shoot — nail the arc before time runs out.
basketball-2012-google-doodle.controls: Shoot the ball or or

birth-of-hip-hop-doodle-game.title: Birth Of Hip Hop Doodle Game
birth-of-hip-hop-doodle-game.description:
In August 1973, DJ Kool Herc threw a back-to-school party in the Bronx and invented an entirely new form of music. This Google Doodle celebrates that moment by handing you two turntables and a mixer. Kool walks you through the basics — how to isolate the breakbeat, cross-fade between records, and layer sounds into something electric.

Practise your scratches, line up the drops, and assemble a mix from a library of classic breaks. The crowd on the dance floor responds to every move you make — get it right and the entire block is moving. A music-history lesson disguised as a ridiculously fun game.
intro: DJ Kool Herc threw the party that started hip-hop — and this Doodle puts you on the decks.
Learn to scratch, cross-fade, and build a beat that moves the crowd.
birth-of-hip-hop-doodle-game.controls: Slide the mixer lever or

blob-opera-google-game.title: Blob Opera
blob-opera-google-game.description:
Blob Opera was built by Google Arts & Culture using machine learning trained on real opera singers. The four Blobs — soprano, mezzo, tenor, and bass — harmonise automatically no matter where you move them, making it impossible to produce anything that doesn't sound at least a little magnificent.

Drag a Blob upward to raise pitch, sideways to shift the vowel sound. Move multiple Blobs at once to sculpt chords that fill the room. You don't need a single music lesson to enjoy it, but trained musicians quickly discover surprising depths. Record your masterpiece and share it with the world.
intro: Four colourful blobs love opera — and you're their conductor.
Drag them up, down, and sideways to shape pitch, vowel, and harmony in real time.
blob-opera-google-game.controls: Conduct the Blob Opera singers

block-champ-10x10.title: Block Champ (10x10)
block-champ-10x10.description:
Block Champ combines the calm of a jigsaw with the tension of Tetris. Three randomly shaped pieces appear at once — you choose where and when to place each one. Fill a complete row or column and it vanishes, freeing up precious space. Fill two at once for a combo bonus.

Unlike Tetris, nothing falls automatically. Every decision is deliberate, which means there is no one to blame when the board fills up except yourself. The pieces keep coming until placement becomes impossible — see how long your strategic streak can last.
intro: Drop blocks onto a 10×10 grid, fill rows and columns to clear them, and never fill the board.
The longer your streak, the bigger the bonus — but one bad placement ends everything.
block-champ-10x10.controls: Place pieces on the grid

block-puzzle-2020.title: Block Puzzle 2020
block-puzzle-2020.description:
Block Puzzle 2020 is the slower, more meditative cousin of Tetris. Blocks of various shapes slide onto the board at your pace — no timers, no falling pieces, just you and the grid. Arrange them so complete rows and columns vanish, freeing up room for what comes next.

The satisfying snap of a perfect clear keeps pulling you back for another attempt. The real challenge is planning ahead: a shape that solves one problem often creates three others. A brilliant puzzle for focused players who like to think before they act.
intro: Slot colourful geometric blocks into a grid and clear lines to survive.
Nothing falls automatically — every placement is a choice you own completely.
block-puzzle-2020.controls: Drag and drop a piece or

boba-bubble-tea.title: Boba Bubble Tea
boba-bubble-tea.description:
Bubble tea — or boba — conquered the world from a Taiwanese tea stand in the 1980s, and this Google Doodle celebrates its global journey with a charming interactive quiz. Learn about the origins of tapioca pearls, the art of shaking a perfect cup, and the dizzying variety of flavours that turned a humble drink into an international phenomenon.

Each stage guides you through the story of boba — from its roots in Taichung to the creative toppings that define modern bubble tea culture. Tap along, discover fun facts, and watch the animations bring every fizzy, chewy, sweet sip to life. A refreshing celebration of one of the world's most iconic drinks.
intro: Google celebrates the world's favourite Taiwanese drink with a bubble tea making Doodle.
Shake, mix, and pour ingredients in the right order to satisfy every customer.
boba-bubble-tea.controls:
Brew the bubble tea or
Toggle music or

capyloop-snack.title: Capyloop Snack
capyloop-snack.description:
Capyloop is powered by an insatiable appetite and the laws of momentum. Launch the capybara in a direction and it rolls until something stops it — meaning you must think several bounces ahead before committing to any move. Every snack collected feeds both the capybara and your score.

Early levels teach the bounce mechanics gently; later puzzles require careful multi-step planning that rivals chess in its demand for foresight. A wonderfully original puzzle wrapped around the most lovable creature in the animal kingdom.
intro: A round, very hungry capybara needs your help rolling through a snack-filled maze.
Plan the route first — the capybara won't stop until it hits a wall.
capyloop-snack.controls: Guide the capybara to the fruit

car-football.title: Car Football
car-football.description:
Car Football asks a simple question: what if football players were replaced by rocket-powered cars? The answer involves a lot of sideways skidding, ball-chasing chaos, and the occasional accidental own goal. Physics make every match feel unpredictable in the best way.

Master the boost, judge the angles, and practise the aerial flip that sends the ball arcing into the top corner. The more control you develop over your car, the more creative your play becomes — but even beginners score spectacular goals by pure accident.
intro: Forget boots — grab a set of wheels and play football at full throttle.
Rocket-power your car into the ball and smash it past the goalkeeper.
car-football.controls:
Player 1
Player 1 — drive the car

catpad.title: Catpad
catpad.description:
Catpad gathers a diverse set of mini-games under one roof, with a cat as the hero of every challenge. One moment you're timing precise jumps over obstacles; the next you're solving a quick spatial puzzle or racing against a countdown. Each game is short enough to try in a spare minute but polished enough to keep you attempting it for an hour.

Finish every challenge to unlock the full catalogue, and replay your favourites to beat your own records. The cat protagonist brings a consistent thread of personality and charm to what is essentially a wonderful sampler of browser game genres.
intro: A collection of bite-sized mini-games, all starring the most capable cat in the world.
Reflexes, puzzle-solving, and feline charm — packed into one entertaining package.
catpad.controls:
Guide the cat's paw or
Restart
Pause / Menu

celebrating-johann-sebastian-bach.title: Celebrating Johann Sebastian Bach
celebrating-johann-sebastian-bach.description:
This Doodle uses a machine learning model trained on Bach's entire catalogue to turn any melody into a four-part baroque chorale. You don't need to read music — just click the notes on the staff that feel right, press Harmonize, and the AI instantly writes soprano, alto, tenor, and bass parts around your tune.

The results are often stunning and always surprising. Experiment with dissonant intervals or chromatic runs and hear how the model reacts. It's both a playable game and a genuine window into how artificial intelligence understands musical grammar — a remarkable achievement hidden inside a birthday Doodle.
intro: Google's AI harmonises your melody in the style of Bach — live, in seconds.
Hum a tune, pick some notes, and watch machine learning compose four-part baroque counterpoint.
celebrating-johann-sebastian-bach.controls: Arrange the melody notes or

celebrating-lake-xochimilco.title: Celebrating Lake Xochimilco
celebrating-lake-xochimilco.description:
Lake Xochimilco is a UNESCO World Heritage Site on the outskirts of Mexico City, home to floating gardens called chinampas and one of the world's most unusual animals: the axolotl, a salamander that retains its juvenile features for its entire life. This Doodle turns the lake into a serene photography adventure.

Steer your canoe through the winding waterways, keeping your camera ready for every creature that surfaces. Collect all the species in your guide, learn a surprising fact about each one, and appreciate why this fragile ecosystem deserves protection. Beautiful, educational, and genuinely calming.
intro: Paddle through Mexico's ancient canal system and photograph its extraordinary wildlife.
The axolotl awaits — snap every species to complete your field guide.
celebrating-lake-xochimilco.controls:
Snap a photo or
Close the scene

celebrating-pani-puri.title: Celebrating Pani Puri
celebrating-pani-puri.description:
Pani puri — crispy hollow shells filled with spiced water, chickpeas, and chutney — is one of India's most iconic street foods, and this Google Doodle celebrates it with a joyful interactive experience. Each round presents you with a plate of empty puri shells and a selection of fillings and flavoured waters. Choose the right combination of ingredients for each puri to match the recipe and serve a perfect batch.

As you progress, the recipes grow more complex and the selection of fillings expands, testing how well you know your tamarind from your mint water. The vibrant art and playful animations make every successful plate feel like a celebration. A delicious, colourful tribute to Indian street food culture that will leave you craving the real thing.
intro: Assemble the perfect pani puri by choosing the right fillings and flavoured water.
Each round introduces new ingredients — master every recipe to become a street food legend.
celebrating-pani-puri.controls: Pick puri groups

celebrating-petanque.title: Celebrating Pétanque
celebrating-petanque.description:
Pétanque is played on sun-baked gravel in the south of France, but this Google Doodle brings the boules to any screen. The goal is elegantly simple: toss your metal ball as close to the tiny cochonnet target as possible, while knocking your opponent's balls far out of scoring range.

Set the power, angle your throw, and apply backspin to stop the ball exactly where you want it. Online play pits you against players from around the world — and every game carries the quiet tension of a close-fought game in a Provençal village square.
intro: The French art of boules arrives in your browser — aim close, knock rivals away.
Master the spin and judge the terrain to dominate the worldwide leaderboard.
celebrating-petanque.controls:
Aim and throw the boule or
Pick a reaction emoji
Open emoji

chamion-island-games.title: Champion Island Games
chamion-island-games.description:
Champion Island Games is the most ambitious Google Doodle ever made. Built for the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, it casts you as Lucky, a ninja cat who arrives on a mythological Japanese island to compete in seven Olympic disciplines: table tennis, skateboarding, archery, marathon, artistic swimming, rugby, and climbing.

Each sport has its own mini-game mechanics and its own legendary champion to defeat. Between events, explore the island's pixel-art landscapes, talk to NPCs, and uncover hidden side quests. With over 15 minutes of content and a full soundtrack, this Doodle plays more like a genuine video game than a throwaway internet treat.
intro: A full retro RPG Doodle built for the Tokyo Olympics — seven sports, one legendary island.
Defeat every champion to claim the title of Island Hero.
chamion-island-games.controls:
Move Lucky the cat or
Interact / skip dialogue / start a round

chess1.title: Chess
chess1.description:
Chess is a complete, properly implemented browser chess game. Choose your side, set the AI difficulty from beginner to challenging, and play out a full game with all the rules enforced — en passant, castling, promotion, the lot. Or sit across the desk from a friend and take turns at the same screen.

Study the board, develop your pieces early, and don't neglect your king's safety. Whether you're learning the game for the first time or sharpening your tactics before a tournament, this is the cleanest and most accessible chess experience available without installing anything.
intro: Full chess in your browser — play a smart AI or challenge a friend locally.
Sharpen your opening theory, control the centre, and go for the king.
chess1.controls: Drag a piece to move it

chicken-invaders.title: Chicken invaders
chicken-invaders.description:
The galaxy's greatest threat turned out to be poultry. Rows of chicken-shaped alien ships descend in classic Space Invaders formation, pelting your fighter with drumsticks, eggs, and the occasional roast. Slide back and forth across the screen, targeting the weak spots in each formation before they reach the planet below.

Power-ups drop from defeated chickens — grab them quickly to upgrade your firepower and clear the screen faster. Boss chickens arrive at the end of each wave with inflated health and infuriated attack patterns. Humanity's future is in your hands. Try not to get scrambled.
intro: Alien chickens have invaded the galaxy — and only your fighter stands between them and the universe.
Blast through formations of feathered foes and dodge the drumsticks they throw back.
chicken-invaders.controls: Pilot your spaceship

chinese-new-year-snake-game.title: Chinese New Year - Snake Game
chinese-new-year-snake-game.description:
Chinese New Year Snake swaps the classic dots for holiday sweets: dumplings, tangerines, and red envelopes scatter across the board as your serpentine dragon slides through the celebration. Collect as many treats as possible within the time limit to maximise your Lunar New Year score.

The snake grows with every morsel consumed, and the longer it gets the harder it becomes to steer without biting your own tail. A familiar format transformed by festive visuals and a delightfully traditional soundtrack into something fresh and charming.
intro: A festive Snake dressed up for the Lunar New Year — collect treats, avoid your own tail.
The dragon-snake grows with every sweet it swallows, making every move more perilous.
chinese-new-year-snake-game.controls: Steer the snake or

coffee-craze-sorting-game.title: Coffee Craze - Sorting Game
coffee-craze-sorting-game.description:
Coffee Craze is a colour-sorting puzzle dressed in a cosy coffee-shop theme. Each level presents you with a set of tall glasses filled with layers of different coloured liquids. Your goal is to pour liquid from one glass to another, one colour at a time, until every glass contains a single pure colour from top to bottom.

The catch is simple but devious: you can only pour onto a matching colour or into an empty glass, and the glasses fill up fast. Later levels add more colours, taller containers, and fewer empty glasses to work with, demanding careful planning several moves ahead. A relaxing yet surprisingly brain-bending puzzle that rewards patience and spatial thinking.
intro: Pour coloured liquids between glasses until each one holds a single pure colour.
Think ahead — one careless pour can leave you stuck with no moves left.
coffee-craze-sorting-game.controls: Sort the coffee boxes or

craft-block-world-building.title: Craft Block World Building
craft-block-world-building.description:
Craft Block World Building is a voxel sandbox with no rules and no time limits — just an infinite canvas of colourful cubic blocks waiting to be arranged into something wonderful. Place blocks individually, paint them in any hue, and stack them as high as the engine allows.

Start with a simple hut and let ambition take over. Unlock new block types as your construction grows in scale, and add roads, parks, and towers until you've built the city of your dreams. A genuinely meditative building experience that rewards patience and creativity in equal measure.
intro: Build anything you can imagine one colourful block at a time.
Place, paint, and stack voxels to grow a tiny house into a sprawling city.
craft-block-world-building.controls:
Move the builder
Look around
Jump
Place a block
Remove a block

dice-roll-protect-the-relic.title: Dice Roll: Protect the Relic
dice-roll-protect-the-relic.description:
Your ancient tower cradles a priceless relic, and a very determined monster army is on its way. Roll the dice to generate spell points, then spend them on defensive abilities, attack spells, and barrier upgrades before the next wave crashes through your gates. The dice give you options — strategy decides which ones to use.

Each monster type demands a different response: slow tankier enemies before they reach the wall, blast fragile fast ones before they slip past your defences. The waves escalate in size and variety, forcing you to adapt your strategy mid-battle. A clever fusion of dice mechanics and tower defence tension.
intro: Roll the dice, deploy your spells, and hold the line against an incoming monster horde.
Every roll is a gamble — every decision shapes whether the relic survives.
dice-roll-protect-the-relic.controls: Roll the dice

dino-swords.title: Dino Swords
dino-swords.description:
Dino Swords starts exactly like the Chrome offline game you know, then escalates wonderfully: weapons appear on the ground mid-sprint and your dinosaur picks them up automatically. A sword slices through cacti. A shield absorbs a pterodactyl hit. An umbrella does something completely unexpected.

Each run feels different depending on which weapons appear and in which order. The original weapon-free classic mode is also available for purists, but why would you choose that when there's a laser cannon waiting just past the next cactus?
intro: The Chrome dino is back — and this time it's armed to the teeth.
Pick up swords, clubs, and shields as you sprint; each weapon changes how you handle obstacles.
dino-swords.controls:
Jump or
Equip a weapon or or or or or or

dinosaur.title: Google Dinosaur Game
dinosaur.description:
When your internet connection drops, Chrome shows you a small pixelated T-Rex in the desert. Press Space to start running — and suddenly you're playing one of the most addictive games ever made. Jump over cacti, duck under pterodactyls, and keep your survival streak alive as the speed steadily climbs.

The game secretly caps at a score of 99,999 but very few humans have ever seen it. No checkpoints, no lives, no save states — every run starts from zero. A perfect illustration of why simple mechanics and pure skill expression create timeless games. Play it here whenever you want, no broken internet required.
intro: Chrome's legendary offline dino — jump cacti, duck pterodactyls, and survive forever.
A tiny homage to the pre-internet era, hidden inside every Chrome browser.
dinosaur.controls:
Jump or
Duck

doctor-who-doodle.title: Doctor Who Doodle
doctor-who-doodle.description:
Created to celebrate the 50th anniversary of Doctor Who, this Google Doodle sends you on a puzzle adventure across six distinct eras of the show. Each world scrambles a segment of the TARDIS into pieces and hides them across a series of logic puzzles. Find the right fragment and slot it back into place to restore the time machine.

The art direction shifts with each era — from the black-and-white 1960s to the full-colour modern series — while familiar sound effects and music reward fans who know their Doctors. Whether you're a lifelong Whovian or a curious newcomer, the puzzles are satisfying and the atmosphere is perfect. Allons-y.
intro: The TARDIS has been torn apart across time — piece it back together before the Daleks notice.
Journey through six puzzle worlds spanning the entire Doctor Who timeline.
doctor-who-doodle.controls: Move the Doctor or or

doodle-celebrating-loteria.title: Doodle Celebrating Lotería
doodle-celebrating-loteria.description:
Lotería is played at Mexican fiestas, family gatherings, and community events across the world. A caller draws illustrated cards one by one — El Gallo, La Sirena, El Mundo — and players mark matching symbols on their personal boards. First to complete a row, column, or diagonal shouts Lotería and wins the round.

Google's Doodle adds online multiplayer and gorgeous animated card art inspired by traditional woodblock printing. The pace is quick, the cards arrive with short poems read aloud, and the community chat adds genuine electricity to every near-miss. A vibrant celebration of Mexican culture wrapped in a game everyone can play.
intro: Mexico's iconic card game — halfway between Bingo and a storytelling tradition.
Match symbols on your board before your online rivals do and shout Lotería!
doodle-celebrating-loteria.controls: Place a bean on your card or

doodle-celebrating-mbira.title: Doodle Celebrating Mbira
doodle-celebrating-mbira.description:
The mbira dzavadzimu is a sacred instrument of the Shona people of Zimbabwe, traditionally used to communicate with ancestral spirits during all-night ceremonies called biras. Its distinctive shimmering tone comes from metal tines plucked by the thumbs, often with a gourd resonator attached to amplify the sound.

This Google Doodle lets you play the mbira yourself: click or tap the keys to pluck individual notes and build up simple melodies. Historical context is woven through the experience, connecting the music you make to the culture it comes from. A rare and beautiful lesson in a living musical tradition.
intro: The mbira — Zimbabwe's ancient thumb piano — carries centuries of spiritual meaning.
Pluck its keys in this Doodle and discover a musical tradition unlike any other.
doodle-celebrating-mbira.controls:
Pluck the mbira keys or
Pause / Menu

doodle-clara-rockmore.title: Doodle Clara Rockmore
doodle-clara-rockmore.description:
The theremin is one of the oldest electronic instruments — two antennae that sense the position of the performer's hands and translate movement into pitch and volume. Clara Rockmore was a violin prodigy who mastered it so completely that she could perform full classical concerts, her hands carving precise notes out of empty air.

This Doodle recreates that experience in a browser. Move your mouse up and down to change pitch, left and right to change volume, and navigate the note guide on screen to perform a complete piece in Rockmore's memory. Beautiful, eerie, and unlike any other game in this collection.
intro: Clara Rockmore was the theremin's greatest virtuoso — an instrument played without touching it.
Wave your cursor through the air to perform a haunting melody in this tribute Doodle.
doodle-clara-rockmore.controls: Play the theremin melody or

doodle-cricket-game.title: Doodle Cricket
doodle-cricket-game.description:
Doodle Cricket was released to mark the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy. Your team of animated cricketers faces off against a squad of comically enthusiastic insects: a spider keeps goal, a grasshopper bowls, and a beetle offers detailed post-match analysis. The stakes feel low; the fun is high.

Timing is everything — tap just as the ball reaches the sweet spot of the bat to launch it over the boundary for six. Mistimed shots result in catches or clean bowled dismissals. Keep your wickets and accumulate as many runs as possible before the innings closes. Tea is provided at the interval.
intro: Google celebrates the ICC Champions Trophy with an insect cricket match.
Tap at the perfect moment to bat — rack up sixes before getting bowled out.
doodle-cricket-game.controls: Play a shot or or

doodle-crossword-puzzle.title: Doodle Crossword Puzzle
doodle-crossword-puzzle.description:
Google's crossword Doodle is a fully functional classic crossword with a clean interface that feels like a Sunday broadsheet, minus the inky fingers. Click a square, read the clue, and type your answer. The grid fills in around you as correct letters appear in their slots, revealing crossing answers that unlock other parts of the puzzle.

Use the check function to verify individual letters if you're stuck, or challenge yourself to solve it clean for the full satisfaction of a no-hint complete. The clues cover culture, science, sport, and wordplay — a proper test of general knowledge and lateral thinking.
intro: A proper newspaper-style crossword delivered straight to your browser.
Work through clever clues across and down — no pencil, no eraser, just your brain.
doodle-crossword-puzzle.controls:
Select a clue and line
Flip clue direction or
Navigate the crossword

doodle-earth-day-2020.title: Doodle Earth Day 2020
doodle-earth-day-2020.description:
Bees are responsible for pollinating roughly one third of all the food humanity eats, yet their populations are in sharp decline. This Google Doodle for Earth Day 2020 puts you inside a bee's flight path: navigate from flower to flower collecting pollen, avoid obstacles, and carry your load back to the hive safely.

Between levels, short facts explain the extraordinary importance of pollinators to global food security. It's a gentle, beautiful game with a quiet urgency behind it — a reminder that the smallest creatures often carry the heaviest responsibility for keeping the planet alive.
intro: Help a bee pollinate flowers and protect the ecosystem in this Earth Day Doodle.
Every flower visited teaches a lesson about why bees matter more than almost anything.
doodle-earth-day-2020.controls: Fly the bee

doodle-googles-15th-birthday.title: Doodle Google's 15th Birthday
doodle-googles-15th-birthday.description:
Released in 2013 to celebrate fifteen years of Google, this birthday Doodle hands you a stick and sets you loose on a spinning piñata. The Google logo letters dance and sway as you time your swings to crack open the piñata and send candy flying across the screen.

The longer you keep swinging without missing, the faster the piñata spins and the more points you earn. A simple, joyful celebration that captures exactly the kind of sugar-rush excitement that belongs at a 15th birthday party. Short, sweet, and thoroughly Google.
intro: It's Google's 15th birthday and the letters are playing piñata at the party.
Swing for the candy, keep your streak alive, and celebrate in style.
doodle-googles-15th-birthday.controls: Smash the piñata or or

doodle-halloween-2020.title: Doodle Halloween 2020
doodle-halloween-2020.description:
Halloween 2020 sent Magic Cat Academy's beloved wizard cat Momo to the bottom of the ocean, where a new wave of supernatural trouble was brewing. Sea creatures have been possessed by ghosts, and only Momo's spellbook can save them. Swipe the glowing symbols that float above each approaching spirit before it reaches the edge of the screen.

Each ghost type moves at a different speed and requires a different symbol pattern. Boss spirits at the end of each level absorb multiple hits. The underwater art is gorgeous, the music builds perfectly, and the satisfying whoosh of a well-timed banishment never gets old. A worthy sequel to the 2016 original.
intro: Magic Cat Momo dives underwater this Halloween to banish ocean ghosts.
Swipe matching symbols to exorcise spirits before they overwhelm the deep.
doodle-halloween-2020.controls:
Draw a spell or
Pause

doodle-halloween-2022.title: Doodle  Halloween 2022
doodle-halloween-2022.description:
Halloween 2022 is a frantic multiplayer soul-collecting race. As a ghost floating through a spooky landscape, you must round up wandering spirits and shepherd them back to your base. The faster you move and the smarter you route, the more you gather before the clock expires.

Other players are doing the same thing in the same arena — the ghost who delivers the most souls wins. Obstacles and rival ghosts add chaos to every run. The art style is playful, the competition is genuine, and the whole thing takes about three minutes — making it one of the most replayable Doodles ever built.
intro: You're a ghost on a mission — collect wandering souls and escort them home before time runs out.
Work with online players to gather more souls than any rival ghost catcher.
doodle-halloween-2022.controls: Move the ghost or or or

doodle-history-of-pizza.title: History of Pizza
doodle-history-of-pizza.description:
Pizza is far older than most people realise. This educational Google Doodle traces its evolution from ancient Greek and Roman flatbreads through to the Neapolitan pizza of the 1800s and the New York slice of the 20th century. At each stop you step behind the counter and prepare a pie in that era's distinctive style.

The toppings, dough thickness, and oven type change with every period. Learn historical facts about each evolution as you cook, and appreciate how a humble bread-and-topping combination became the world's favourite food. Informative, delicious-looking, and genuinely fun.
intro: Pizza's history stretches from ancient flatbreads to Neapolitan mastery — and this Doodle covers every era.
Play as a pizzaiolo at each stop in history, topping pies in the correct style.
doodle-history-of-pizza.controls: Slice the pizza or

doodle-jump.title: Doodle Jump unblockeds online
doodle-jump.description:
Doodle Jump is a vertical platformer with deceptively simple controls. The Doodler bounces automatically — you just tilt or tap to steer left and right, aiming for the next platform before the previous one disappears off the bottom of the screen. Miss a platform, fall too far, and it's over.

Monsters, UFOs, and black holes lurk between the platforms, but your Doodler can shoot them — tap the screen above to fire. Power-ups include jetpacks, springs, and propeller caps that send you rocketing upward. The score climbs linearly with height, and the global leaderboard is ruthlessly competitive. One of the most genuinely addictive mobile games ever made, and just as compelling in a browser.
intro: A little alien bounces upward forever — your job is to keep it from falling.
Jump, shoot, and grab power-ups as platforms get scarcer and enemies get meaner.
doodle-jump.controls:
Steer the alien
Fire or or

doodle-jump-2.title: Doodle Jump 2
doodle-jump-2.description:
Doodle Jump 2 takes the formula into the cosmos. The Doodler dons a spacesuit and the notebook-paper aesthetic gives way to crisp space environments: asteroid fields, alien installations, and nebulae that serve as backdrops for the same relentless upward bounce.

New platform types include moving tiles, crumbling ones, and gravity-flipping pads that send you shooting in unexpected directions. Enemy designs are more varied and the power-up roster has grown. The core satisfaction — that one more bounce, one more metre — remains completely intact. A sequel that earns its place.
intro: The bouncing alien blasts off into space in this stylish sequel.
New platforms, fresh enemies, and the same irresistible upward pull.
doodle-jump-2.controls:
Guide the alien
Fire or or or

doodle-jump-3.title: Doodle Jump 3
doodle-jump-3.description:
Doodle Jump returns with the same irresistible bounce-and-climb formula that made the original a mobile legend. The Doodler launches off green platforms, springs from trampolines, and rides rocket packs through an ever-scrolling vertical world filled with monsters, UFOs, and black holes that will swallow your run whole.

Tilt left and right to land on each platform and shoot upward at enemies blocking your path. Every attempt teaches you something new — which platforms crumble, which monsters dodge your shots, and exactly how far you can stretch a risky gap. The leaderboard is always watching, and beating your personal best never gets old.
intro: Bounce ever higher through a gauntlet of monsters, moving platforms, and jetpack rides.
One missed platform ends the run — how high can you climb before gravity wins?
doodle-jump-3.controls:
Tilt the alien left or right
Fire or or

doodle-kids-coding.title: Doodle Kids Coding
doodle-kids-coding.description:
Kids Coding is Google's introduction to computational thinking for young players. The Easter Bunny needs to deliver eggs to every house in the village, but the route must be programmed in advance using drag-and-drop command blocks — move forward, turn left, turn right, repeat.

Early puzzles are solved with three or four commands. Later stages introduce loops and conditionals that demand genuine logical thinking. Children complete each level without realising they've just written their first algorithm. Parents and teachers will appreciate the elegance of the lesson; kids will just enjoy helping the bunny.
intro: Help the Easter Bunny deliver eggs by writing a simple program — without a single line of code.
Sequence commands, add loops, and discover the fundamentals of programming through play.
doodle-kids-coding.controls: Drag coding blocks into the sequence

doodle-ludwig-van-beethovens-245th-year.title: Doodle Beethoven
doodle-ludwig-van-beethovens-245th-year.description:
Created for Beethoven's 245th birthday, this Doodle casts you as a desperate stagehand racing to recover the composer's manuscript pages before the curtain rises. Sheets of music flutter down from above and you must catch them in the correct order — wrong sequence means a bum note, missed pages mean silence where there should be symphony.

The game incorporates real Beethoven melodies that play as each correct page is secured, building toward a triumphant complete performance. It's a clever mechanic that teaches musical structure while keeping the gameplay tense and kinetic. Beethoven would have approved — probably.
intro: Beethoven's sheet music has been scattered by the wind — catch every page before the concert.
A musical puzzle celebrating 245 years of the greatest composer who ever lived.
doodle-ludwig-van-beethovens-245th-year.controls: Arrange the score sheets or

doodle-oskar-fischinger.title: Doodle Oskar Fischinger
doodle-oskar-fischinger.description:
Oskar Fischinger spent decades in pre-war Germany creating abstract films that moved in perfect synchrony with music — geometric shapes that danced, pulsed, and transformed to the beat. This Google Doodle lets you create your own Fischinger film using a sequencer grid.

Each row of the grid represents a different instrument; each column is a beat. Paint cells in any pattern, press play, and your composition loops while an abstract animation reacts to the music you've made. Experiment with rhythm, harmony, and density. Even complete non-musicians produce something that looks and sounds surprisingly good.
intro: Paint an animated musical canvas in tribute to Oskar Fischinger, pioneer of visual music.
Arrange coloured cells on a grid and press play to watch your composition come alive.
doodle-oskar-fischinger.controls: Choose shapes and build a composition or

doodle-qixi-festival-chilseok.title: Doodle Qixi Festival / Chilseok
doodle-qixi-festival-chilseok.description:
The Qixi Festival — China's Valentine's Day — is based on the legend of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, two lovers separated by the Milky Way who are allowed to meet only once a year when magpies form a bridge across the stars. This Google Doodle turns that reunion into a puzzle.

Rotate and rearrange star bridge segments to create a continuous path connecting the two lovers. Each stage adds new bridge types and more complex configurations, but the romantic urgency of the story keeps every solution feeling meaningful. A beautiful puzzle with a heart.
intro: Reunite two star-crossed lovers across the Milky Way in this romantic logic Doodle.
Flip bridges and reroute paths through a celestial puzzle to bring them together.
doodle-qixi-festival-chilseok.controls: Guide the magpie birds

doodle-roswells-66th-anniversary.title: Doodle Roswell's 66th Anniversary
doodle-roswells-66th-anniversary.description:
On July 8, 1947, the US Air Force announced it had recovered a crashed flying disc near Roswell, New Mexico. This Google Doodle marks the 66th anniversary of that event with a point-and-click adventure from the alien's perspective. Your ship is in pieces, the locals are deeply suspicious, and the authorities are on their way.

Search the crash site for missing components, use your alien gadgets to blend in with confused townsfolk, and reassemble your UFO before the military cordons off the entire desert. A charming adventure full of period detail and gentle humour about humanity's most enduring conspiracy theory.
intro: A UFO has crashed in Roswell, 1947 — and you're the alien trying to get home.
Collect ship parts, blend in with the suspicious locals, and escape before the government arrives.
doodle-roswells-66th-anniversary.controls: Move the alien or

doodle-scoville.title: Doodle Scoville
doodle-scoville.description:
Wilbur Scoville invented the organoleptic test in 1912 to measure the heat of chilli peppers — a scale still used today. This Doodle celebrates his work with an unlikely action game: you play as a timid sweet pepper who must battle up through the Scoville rankings by fighting progressively hotter opponents.

Each enemy pepper has more heat points than the last, and getting hit sets you on fire. Fortunately, milk bottles appear on the battlefield — grab them to cool down and restore health, exactly as Scoville discovered during his original experiments. Spicy, educational, and oddly cathartic.
intro: Fight your way up the spicy Scoville scale as a mild sweet pepper battling chilli veterans.
Collect milk to soothe the burn, and defeat jalapeños, habaneros, and ghost peppers to win.
doodle-scoville.controls: Hurl ice cream at the pepper or or or or or or or or

doodle-valentines-day.title: Doodle Valentine's Day
doodle-valentines-day.description:
This Valentine's Day Doodle follows a devoted pangolin on an ingredient-gathering quest across beautiful illustrated landscapes. Roll through forests, climb hills, and navigate natural hazards to collect the components for a very special cake — all driven by pure, uncomplicated love.

The controls are simple but the obstacles grow trickier with each stage. The pangolin's determination is infectious, the art is gorgeous, and the whole thing is wrapped in a warmth that makes it one of the most charming Doodles Google has ever released. The cake, when you finally deliver it, is absolutely worth the journey.
intro: A love-struck pangolin needs your help gathering ingredients for the perfect Valentine's cake.
Navigate treacherous terrain, collect every grain, and deliver the gift before the day ends.
doodle-valentines-day.controls:
Move the pangolin or
Jump or or
Dive underwater

doodle-valentines-day-2022.title: Valentine's Day 2022
doodle-valentines-day-2022.description:
Valentine's Day 2022 delivers a satisfying pipe-connection puzzle with a romantic twist. Two hamsters gaze at each other across a screen full of scrambled plumbing. Your job is to rotate individual pipe segments until they form a continuous path from one hamster to the other — creating the tunnel of love they so urgently need.

Early levels are gentle warm-ups; later stages use complex branching networks where multiple dead ends must be untangled before the correct route reveals itself. A sweet, clever puzzle that earns its happy ending.
intro: Two hamsters are in love — but a maze of disconnected pipes stands between them.
Rotate segments to create a continuous path and reunite the lovestruck rodents.
doodle-valentines-day-2022.controls:
Rotate a pipe segment or
Toggle mute or

eiji-tsuburayas-birthday.title: Eiji Tsuburaya’s Birthday
eiji-tsuburayas-birthday.description:
Eiji Tsuburaya was a genius of practical effects who built the miniature Tokyo that Godzilla destroyed in 1954, and later created Ultraman — the superhero who defined Japanese tokusatsu television. This Doodle celebrates his 114th birthday by putting you in the director's chair of his legendary studio.

Each scene presents a kaiju encounter that needs specific effects: a building that crumbles on cue, an explosion that hits its mark, a monster that moves convincingly through a miniature city. Arrange your cameras and props, call action, and watch the clip play back. A loving tribute to the man who made movie monsters real.
intro: Direct the special effects scenes that defined Japanese cinema — the Eiji Tsuburaya way.
Position cameras, cue explosions, and bring kaiju battles to life in miniature.
eiji-tsuburayas-birthday.controls: Trigger the action or

feud.title: Google Feud
feud.description:
Google Feud is based on a devastating insight: Google's autocomplete suggestions reveal what millions of people are actually thinking about, and the results are simultaneously predictable and completely baffling. Your challenge is to correctly guess the top ten completions for prompts like 'why is my cat...' or 'how do I tell my boss...'

Type confidently, hit enter, and watch as your obviously correct answer fails to appear anywhere on the list. The gap between what you think people search for and what they actually search for is the entire joke — and the joke is always on you. Genuinely funny, endlessly surprising, and a brilliant time capsule of the internet's collective consciousness.
intro: Finish Google's autocomplete suggestions before the clock — it's harder and funnier than it looks.
The more you search, the better you'll do. Or maybe not.
feud.controls:
Browse categories
Type your guess
Submit answer

garden-gnomes.title: Garden Gnomes
garden-gnomes.description:
Garden Gnomes was released in 2018 to celebrate the 150th anniversary of the garden gnome. The gameplay is a perfect physics fling: set the angle, set the power, pull back the catapult, and watch your gnome sail majestically through the air. Trampolines strategically placed along the flight path can add significant extra distance if you hit them right.

Collect coins mid-flight to unlock better launch equipment and gnomes with improved aerodynamics. Every run brings the satisfying question of whether this time you've finally found the perfect arc. Simple to understand, impossible to put down. One of the most purely enjoyable physics-flinger games on the internet.
intro: Load a garden gnome into a giant catapult and launch it as far as humanly possible.
Bounce off trampolines, collect coins mid-flight, and beat your personal distance record.
garden-gnomes.controls: Launch the gnome or or

gold-digger-frvr.title: Gold Digger FRVR
gold-digger-frvr.description:
Gold Digger FRVR is a mining adventure with a satisfying upgrade loop. Guide your drill bit down through layers of rock, soil, and caverns, steering around worthless gravel to strike gold, silver, and gems. Every resource you extract is worth money — spend it on a bigger drill, a better engine, or a larger haul capacity.

The fuel gauge creates constant tension: go too deep without enough fuel and you're stranded underground with a full hold of treasure you can't spend. Learn the maps, plan your descent, and master the art of profitable extraction. Deeply satisfying in a way that few games manage.
intro: Drill through layers of earth, extract gold and silver, and race back to the surface before you're stranded.
Upgrade your rig between runs to dig deeper, haul more, and find buried treasure.
gold-digger-frvr.controls: Dig and navigate or

google-cat-game.title: Google Cat Game
google-cat-game.description:
Google's beloved Halloween Doodle stars Momo, a brave little black cat defending the Magic Cat Academy from an invasion of mischievous ghosts. Each phantom floats toward you bearing a glowing symbol — quickly trace that exact shape with your mouse or finger to cast a spell and banish it before it reaches you.

As the night deepens, ghosts arrive faster and their symbols grow more intricate, demanding razor-sharp reflexes and precise drawing. Chain multiple banishments together for screen-clearing combo bursts that rack up massive points. With five spooky levels and an unforgettable boss fight, this 2016 Doodle remains one of Google's most polished and replayable creations.
intro: Momo the cat must defend the Magic Cat Academy from waves of symbol-bearing ghosts.
Trace each ghost's symbol to cast a spell — chain combos and survive the night.
google-cat-game.controls: Trace a spell symbol or

google-doodle-football.title: Google Doodle Football
google-doodle-football.description:
Google's football Doodle for the 2012 London Olympics puts you between the posts for a penalty shootout that never ends. Wave after wave of animated players step up to take their shot — your job is to pick a direction and dive at exactly the right moment to push the ball wide.

Early rounds are forgiving; the ball moves at a pace that allows careful reading. As the shootout progresses, pace and placement improve until you're making reaction-time saves that feel genuinely athletic. A simple game made compelling by the universal tension of the penalty kick.
intro: Dive and save penalties in Google's London 2012 Olympics goalkeeper Doodle.
Read the kicker's body language and choose your direction before it's too late.
google-doodle-football.controls:
Move the goalkeeper or
Leap up or or

google-maps-snake.title: Google Maps Snake
google-maps-snake.description:
Released as an April Fools joke in 2019, Google Maps Snake replaced the satellite view with a classic Snake game set inside recognisable city maps. Choose London, Tokyo, Sydney, São Paulo, Cairo, or San Francisco, then guide your train along the streets collecting passengers without running into the growing tail behind you.

Landmarks, street layouts, and local icons are rendered in charming miniature along the route. The cities get busier and the streets get narrower as your train grows, making the endgame feel surprisingly tense. One of the cleverest uses of a familiar map platform ever conceived.
intro: Play Snake inside Google Maps — pick a city and guide your growing train through real streets.
Collect passengers in London, São Paulo, Cairo, and more without crossing your own path.
google-maps-snake.controls: Steer the train-snake or or or

google-santa-tracker.title: Google Santa Tracker
google-santa-tracker.description:
Google Santa Tracker opens its doors each December with a new mini-game or interactive activity every day leading up to Christmas Eve. Wrap gifts against the clock, sort letters by destination, teach elves to code, launch snowballs at targets — each day brings something different and seasonally appropriate.

On Christmas Eve the tracker goes live, plotting Santa's real-time journey across every time zone as gifts are delivered to billions of children. Whether you're a child counting down the days or an adult who refuses to let December be boring, Santa Tracker has something delightful for every visit.
intro: Help Santa prepare for Christmas with festive mini-games and track his global journey.
Wrap presents, sort candy, code elves, and more across a full December advent calendar.
google-santa-tracker.controls: Click to interact or

growmi.title: Growmi
growmi.description:
Growmi is a puzzle game built around one beautiful mechanic: your snake-like creature can extend its body to fill empty tiles, and must do so completely before the exit unlocks. Simple in theory — in practice, filling every tile without getting stuck in a dead end requires thinking several steps ahead on every move.

Walls, gaps, and locked doors appear in later levels, forcing you to reconsider your approach entirely. The satisfaction of completing a particularly tricky room is enormous. Growmi is the kind of puzzle game you think about in the shower, working out the solution that eluded you before you sat back down at the screen.
intro: A snake-creature trapped in a dungeon must stretch to fill every tile — then find the exit.
Each room adds new obstacles that demand careful route planning and creative thinking.
growmi.controls:
Steer Growmi or
Undo the last step or
Restart the level
Shrink
Open the map
Pause / Menu

halloween.title: Google Halloween
halloween.description:
Magic Cat Academy is one of the most acclaimed Google Doodles ever created. Momo is a cat wizard defending her school from an onslaught of ghosts that advance from the right side of the screen. Each ghost wears a symbol — a straight line, a star, a zigzag — and you must swipe that symbol before it reaches Momo.

Five boss stages punctuate the adventure, each requiring longer combo patterns and faster reactions. The soundtrack swells as the waves intensify. Successfully clearing the final boss with the full stack of ghosts bearing down on you is one of the most satisfying moments in browser gaming. Play it every Halloween. Play it in June. Just play it.
intro: Magic Cat Momo faces five ghost bosses — draw their symbols fast to defeat them.
Google's beloved Halloween 2016 Doodle, as replayable today as the day it launched.
halloween.controls: Trace a spell symbol or

handless-millionaire.title: Handless-Millionaire
handless-millionaire.description:
Handless Millionaire presents the most direct risk/reward scenario in gaming. A guillotine chops rhythmically. A pile of banknotes sits just beyond the blade. All you have to do is reach through, grab the money, and pull your hand back before the next chop. The prize money increases with every successful grab — as does the speed of the blade.

The game is cruel, hilarious, and physically uncomfortable in a way that few browser games manage. Your hesitation costs you money; your overconfidence costs you worse. A deeply silly premise executed with perfect mechanical precision. Not recommended if you're squeamish.
intro: A guillotine blade swings back and forth — the cash is on the other side.
Time your grab perfectly. The blade does not negotiate.
handless-millionaire.controls:
Move your hand
Grab the cash

hangman-challenge.title: Hangman Challenge
hangman-challenge.description:
Hangman Challenge is the classic word-guessing game cleaned up and brought to the browser with a solid word list and a clear interface. A secret word hides behind blank spaces — every letter you guess either reveals its positions or adds another piece to the hangman drawing. Six wrong guesses and it's over.

Experienced players start with high-frequency consonants: T, N, S, R. Then the common vowels: E, A, I. The pattern that emerges from the correct letters usually suggests the word before you've exhausted your wrong guesses. A timeless vocabulary challenge that rewards both language knowledge and probabilistic thinking.
intro: Guess the hidden word letter by letter before the gallows drawing is complete.
Think about letter frequency, start smart, and don't let the scaffold fill up.
hangman-challenge.controls: Pick a letter or

happy-snakes.title: Happy Snakes
happy-snakes.description:
Happy Snakes is a colourful take on competitive snake gameplay. Your reptile glides through a shared arena collecting glowing particles that make it grow longer. The longer you are, the more of the screen you block — and blocking a rival's path forces them to crash into your body and dissolve into a pile of orbs that you then devour.

Speed boosts are available but cost length, creating a constant tension between staying big and moving fast. The cheerful art style and upbeat music give it a friendlier atmosphere than most competitive snake games, but the competition is just as fierce underneath. A surprisingly deep arena brawler.
intro: Every snake on the planet wants to be the biggest — including yours.
Eat glowing orbs, outmanoeuvre rivals, and cut off their escape routes to feast on their remains.
happy-snakes.controls:
Steer the snake
Burst of speed or

iron-snout-pig-fighting-game.title: Iron Snout - Pig Fighting Game
iron-snout-pig-fighting-game.description:
Iron Snout's premise is elegant: you are a pig and the wolves want you for dinner. Stand your ground in the middle of the screen and fight off every wolf that attacks from the left and right simultaneously. The controls are a single button or directional keys — tap toward a wolf to punch, away to kick, up to flip over them.

Wolves drop weapons as they fall: chainsaws, swords, grenades. Grab them mid-air and turn them against the next attacker. The escalation is relentless and the combo system rewards skilled players with spectacular multi-hit strings. Short sessions, high scores, maximum oink-fu energy.
intro: One pig. Infinite wolves. Iron Snout is a one-button brawler of magnificent intensity.
Kick, punch, backflip, and grab weapons from your enemies' hands before they overwhelm you.
iron-snout-pig-fighting-game.controls:
Fight off the wolves or
Aerial kick or
Break the attack

jerry-lawson.title: Jerry Lawson Game
jerry-lawson.description:
Gerald Anderson 'Jerry' Lawson was a self-taught Black engineer who in 1976 designed the Fairchild Channel F — the first home console to use interchangeable game cartridges. His invention made the modern games industry possible, yet his name remained unknown to most people until recently.

This Google Doodle corrects that oversight with an interactive tribute that puts you in Jerry's workshop. Follow his guidance to program a simple game from scratch using his console's custom chips, then upload your cartridge and see it run. An educational celebration of a genuinely transformative engineer.
intro: Honour Jerry Lawson, inventor of the removable game cartridge, by building your own game.
Step into his 1970s workshop and code a simple game for his pioneering Fairchild Channel F.
jerry-lawson.controls:
Move the character or
Jump
Swap character parts
Open / close the Creator

longcat.title: Longcat
longcat.description:
Longcat is built around one wonderfully ridiculous mechanic: your cat can stretch. Click to extend the feline across the room, filling corridors and triggering pressure plates that open new passages. The cat can grow to extraordinary lengths, but managing that length around tight corners becomes the central challenge.

Each puzzle is a spatial problem that requires thinking about the cat's full body, not just its head. Where will the tail end up when you take this route? Is there enough room to unfurl? A clever, charming puzzle game that proves the best game ideas are sometimes the silliest.
intro: Stretch a rubbery cat through winding mazes by elongating its body to fill every corridor.
Navigate tight corners and trigger platforms — but don't get stuck.
longcat.controls:
Open level select
Move Longcat or or or
Pause / Menu or
Continue after losing or

magic-cat-academy.title: Magic Cat Academy
magic-cat-academy.description:
Magic Cat Academy is consistently ranked among the best Google Doodles ever made. Momo the wizard cat stands at the centre of the screen while ghosts advance from the right, each displaying a symbol — line, X, star, zigzag, circle — above their head. Swipe or draw that symbol quickly enough to banish the ghost before it reaches Momo.

Five levels each culminate in a boss ghost that requires a longer, more complex symbol pattern to defeat. The waves build in intensity and speed, the music shifts to match the urgency, and the final confrontation feels genuinely climactic for a browser game. Absolutely essential play every Halloween.
intro: Draw symbols to defeat a ghost army and defend the Magic Cat Academy.
Google's beloved Halloween 2016 Doodle — five stages, five bosses, one brilliant wizard cat.
magic-cat-academy.controls: Trace a spell symbol or

marble-race-creator.title: Marble Race Creator
marble-race-creator.description:
Marble Race Creator hands you a blank canvas and a toolbox full of ramps, funnels, launchers, and bumpers. Drag pieces onto the track, connect them logically, and press play to watch your marble cascade through the creation. Stars are scattered along every course — a perfect run collects them all.

The satisfaction comes from the iteration: a marble that falls off at the first curve requires a rethink; a gap that seemed like it would work by momentum alone definitely will not. Refine, rebuild, and eventually watch your marble complete a flawless run. Then build a harder one.
intro: Build your own marble run from scratch, then watch physics do the rest.
Design ramps, funnels, and bumpers — optimise the course and beat your own time.
marble-race-creator.controls: Build the marble run track

memory.title: Google Memory Game
memory.description:
Google Memory Game lays sixteen cards face down in a grid. Flip two cards per turn — if they match, they stay revealed and you score; if they don't, they flip back and you need to remember where they were. Clear every pair to complete the board.

Early grids are manageable. Harder difficulties add more cards and more pairs, demanding the kind of focused recall that most people discover they're worse at than they expected. Regular play genuinely improves working memory and concentration. A timeless brain training game wrapped in a clean, satisfying interface.
intro: Flip cards, find matching pairs, and clear the board — Google's classic memory trainer.
The later rounds are genuinely ruthless. Your brain will get the workout it deserves.
memory.controls: Flip a card or

minesweeper.title: Google Minesweeper
minesweeper.description:
Minesweeper has been a staple of computer gaming since the early 1990s. The grid hides a fixed number of mines; every revealed number tells you how many mines are adjacent to that square. Use those numbers to reason about which squares are safe and which must contain mines.

With pure logic you can clear most of the board; occasionally probability and a calculated risk are required. Google's version lets you choose grid size and mine count, ranging from beginner-friendly to fiendishly dense. Click a mine and the whole field explodes satisfyingly. Don't click a mine.
intro: Clear the minefield using logic, deduction, and a steady nerve.
Numbers tell you everything you need to know — use them to find every mine without triggering one.
minesweeper.controls:
Reveal a square
Place a flag

mothers-day-2013-doodle.title: Mother's Day 2013 Doodle
mothers-day-2013-doodle.description:
Google's 2013 Mother's Day Doodle turns the browser into an art studio. A blank card waits for decoration: choose from a selection of stencils, pick your paint colours, layer patterns, and stamp a heartfelt message in your own combination of letter stamps. Every card is unique.

The creative tools are simple enough for young children to use independently but expressive enough to produce genuinely lovely results. When you're happy with your creation, admire the finished card and perhaps screenshot it to share with the person who inspired it. A sweet, low-pressure creative experience.
intro: Design a personalised Mother's Day card using stencils, paint, and decorations.
Pick your colours, stamp your message, and create something beautiful for someone special.
mothers-day-2013-doodle.controls: Choose your options or

mothers-day-2020-doodle.title: Mother's Day 2020 Doodle
mothers-day-2020-doodle.description:
The 2020 Mother's Day Doodle is a digital card maker with a beautiful illustrated style. Select a background scene, choose characters that represent your family, add decorative elements, and type a personal message before the design is complete. The resulting card is entirely your own creation.

The illustration palette covers a remarkable range of family configurations and personal styles, ensuring that everyone can create something genuinely representative. Save your design, share it with mum, and remind her — not just on one Sunday in May, but every day — that she is appreciated. A lovely, thoughtful Doodle.
intro: Mix and match illustrated elements to build the perfect personalised Mother's Day card.
Choose a scene, add characters, layer decorations, and write your message.
mothers-day-2020-doodle.controls:
Select scene elements or
Swap the background or

moto-xm-bike-race-game.title: Moto X3M Bike Race Game
moto-xm-bike-race-game.description:
Moto X3M is a motocross game that treats the concept of a 'safe track' as a quaint historical curiosity. Each level is a gauntlet of ramps, swinging wrecking balls, flaming hoops, and reversed-gravity sections designed specifically to send your rider tumbling in spectacular slow motion.

Master the lean mechanic to nail landings cleanly, use the brakes precisely on descents, and figure out each obstacle's pattern before committing. Beat the target time to earn three stars. The difficulty curve is steep but fair, and the satisfaction of a smooth clean run through a previously murderous level is enormous.
intro: Fly over jumps, loop through rotating saws, and brake hard before impossible drops.
Every crash is spectacular; every clean run is deeply satisfying. Beat par time to progress.
moto-xm-bike-race-game.controls:
Ride the bike or
Restart
Pause

mr-racer.title: Mr Racer
mr-racer.description:
Mr Racer is an endless traffic-dodging game with an upgrade loop that gives every run purpose. Weave between lanes of increasingly dense traffic, scoop up coins scattered across the road, and avoid the collisions that end your run instantly. The road gets faster and more crowded with every passing second.

Coins unlock faster cars, better handling, and special abilities that make subsequent runs more survivable. Each upgrade raises the ceiling of how far you can go, creating a satisfying sense of progression behind the otherwise pure arcade gameplay. A polished, addictive time-eater.
intro: Weave through endless traffic, collect coins, and upgrade your car in this relentless racer.
One wrong swerve sends you back to the start — stay sharp and keep accelerating.
mr-racer.controls:
Steer left / right or
Accelerate or
Brake or
Pause
Honk

no-internet-dinosaur-game-google-chrome-.title: Chrome Dino Game
no-internet-dinosaur-game-google-chrome-.description:
When your Wi-Fi drops and Chrome displays the 'No Internet' page, a small pixelated Tyrannosaurus sits beside the error message. Press Space or tap it and the game begins: the dino charges forward through a desert that scrolls ever faster, and your only tools are a jump and a duck. Hit a cactus or a pterodactyl and it's over.

The game is built into every copy of Chrome — you've probably accidentally started it dozens of times. Play it here intentionally and see how far the simple binary of jump/duck can take you. Deceptively pure, genuinely tense, and responsible for untold hours of productive procrastination worldwide.
intro: The original offline Chrome dino — jump cacti, duck pterodactyls, and run forever.
The score caps at 99,999 — a milestone very few players have ever reached.
no-internet-dinosaur-game-google-chrome-.controls:
Jump or
Duck

odd-bot-out.title: Odd Bot Out
odd-bot-out.description:
Odd Bot Out is a puzzle-platformer with a warm heart. The factory produces identical robots, and our hero is a few centimetres too short for the quality standard — so it's rejected and must find its own way out. Guide the little robot through each chamber by interacting with the environment cleverly.

Push buttons that move platforms, reprogram other robots to act as stepping stones, and use the factory's own machinery against it. The puzzles escalate from simple box-pushing to multi-step chain reactions that require real lateral thinking. The robot's quiet determination and the factory's sterile cruelty make an unexpectedly touching story.
intro: A tiny robot deemed too small for the factory needs your help to escape.
Flip switches, ride platforms, and reprogram other robots to carve a path to the exit.
odd-bot-out.controls:
Move the robot or or
Undo an action
Restart the puzzle

ooze-odyssey-2.title: Ooze Odyssey 2
ooze-odyssey-2.description:
Ooze Odyssey 2 is a puzzle game where your snake-ooze protagonist must eat every edible block in the room before the exit opens — but blocks can only be consumed in a specific sequence determined by colour and position. Eat the wrong thing in the wrong order and you'll find yourself too short to reach the exit or completely boxed in.

Each chamber is a compact logic puzzle that demands spatial reasoning and forward planning. The ooze moves with a satisfying squishy animation, and the increasingly inventive room designs reward players who pause to survey the whole puzzle before making a single move. A hidden gem of a game.
intro: A sentient ooze with an enormous appetite slithers through puzzle chambers, devouring everything.
Eat blocks in the right order to grow long enough to reach the exit.
ooze-odyssey-2.controls:
Player 1
Guide the player or
Restart

pacman.title: Google Pacman
pacman.description:
When Google launched this interactive Pac-Man Doodle in May 2010 for the game's 30th anniversary, the internet collectively stopped working for two days. The entire Google homepage became a fully playable Pac-Man maze with the Google logo as the map — complete with all four original ghost AI behaviours, all the original sound effects, and a hidden two-player mode.

Navigate the maze eating every dot, collect power pellets to turn the ghosts temporarily edible, and chase your high score through level after level. Blinky pursues you directly; Pinky tries to cut you off; Inky and Clyde have their own unpredictable patterns. A perfect game, preserved perfectly, playable here forever.
intro: The full Pac-Man maze built directly into the Google logo — the Doodle that broke the internet.
Gobble dots, eat power pellets, and outsmart Blinky, Pinky, Inky, and Clyde.
pacman.controls: Steer Pac-Man

penguin-fight.title: Penguin Fight
penguin-fight.description:
Penguin Fight is a wave-defence shooter with a satisfying ballistic arc. Snowmen march toward your penguin's igloo from the right side of the screen — aim your snowball launcher, account for the throw arc, and knock them over before they reach the walls. Hit multiple snowmen with a single shot for a combo bonus.

Later waves send snowmen running faster, in formation, or with shields that require precise targeting to bypass. Power-ups drop periodically: rapid fire, explosive snowballs, and a temporary freeze blast that stops the whole wave. Simple enough to pick up instantly, hard enough to keep you focused for much longer than you expect.
intro: Snowmen have invaded the penguin's icy home — hold the line with precisely aimed snowballs.
Each wave is stronger than the last; power-ups help, but your aim carries you through.
penguin-fight.controls: Aim and fire

pixel-art-color-by-numbers.title: Pixel Art - Color by Numbers
pixel-art-color-by-numbers.description:
Pixel Art - Color by Numbers is a digital version of the beloved paint-by-numbers concept, reimagined for the pixel art aesthetic. Each puzzle presents a grid of small cells, each labelled with a number that corresponds to a colour in the palette. Work methodically or leap between colours — either way, an image gradually emerges from the noise.

Puzzles range from simple icons to complex, multi-coloured scenes with hundreds of cells. The satisfaction of the final tile — watching the last empty square fill in and the complete image snap into focus — is deeply pleasant in a way that resists rational explanation. A perfect game for winding down after a busy day.
intro: Fill numbered cells with the matching colour and reveal hidden pixel art images.
Relax, zone out, and watch something beautiful emerge from a grid of numbers.
pixel-art-color-by-numbers.controls:
Fill a cell with color
Zoom in / out

poker-with-friends.title: Poker With Friends
poker-with-friends.description:
Poker With Friends delivers a clean, fully functional Texas Hold'em experience in the browser. Create a private table and share the link with friends, or join an open public table and play against whoever shows up. The standard rules apply: hole cards, community board, betting rounds, showdown.

Without physical tells, online poker becomes a pure battle of probability, position, and bet-sizing. Slow-play a strong hand, fire a well-timed bluff, or fold the mediocre cards that less disciplined players throw chips at. The poker clock ticks. The pot grows. Every hand is a small drama.
intro: Play Texas Hold'em poker with friends or strangers — no download, no account required.
Develop your bluff, read the table, and build the biggest stack.
poker-with-friends.controls: Make your bet or fold

pony-express.title: Pony Express
pony-express.description:
Before the telegraph linked the coasts, the Pony Express operated for 18 months — a relay of riders galloping across 1,900 miles of wilderness to carry mail from Missouri to California. This Google Doodle celebrates those riders with a horseback action game that captures the urgency and romance of the route.

Lean your horse over fences and creek beds, grab mail pouches dangling from posts, and avoid the hazards that line the trail. The Western landscapes scroll beautifully past as the speed increases. A lovely tribute to a short-lived but iconic chapter in American communications history.
intro: Ride hard across the 1860s American frontier, jumping obstacles and delivering the mail.
Collect pouches, outrun bandits, and honour the brave riders of the Pony Express.
pony-express.controls: Switch lanes to dodge obstacles or or

popcorn-box.title: Popcorn Box
popcorn-box.description:
Popcorn Box is a precision challenge disguised as a snack preparation game. A container sits waiting to be filled, a stream of popcorn pours from above, and you must stop the flow at exactly the right moment — fill the box completely without a single kernel spilling over the edge.

Each round changes the size and shape of the container, demanding fresh calculation and restraint. The gap between 'almost full' and 'overflowing' is vanishingly small. It sounds trivial; it is not. One of those games that is impossible to play for just one round.
intro: Pour exactly the right amount of kernels to fill the box to the brim — no more, no less.
The container changes every round; your eye for measurement is the only tool you have.
popcorn-box.controls: Pop the corn kernels or

popcorn-chef-2.title: Popcorn Chef 2
popcorn-chef-2.description:
Popcorn Chef 2 expands on the original's core mechanic with a wider variety of containers and ingredient combinations. Now you're not just pouring kernels — you're adding seasoning, drizzling caramel, and measuring toppings to specific levels with the same demand for precision that defined the first game.

The tolerances for error are tight, and the visual feedback when you nail the fill level exactly right is enormously satisfying. A sequel that earns its existence by expanding the formula without diluting what made it special.
intro: Master precision pouring across new recipes and toppings in this satisfying popcorn sequel.
More containers, more ingredients, and the same ruthless demand for accuracy.
popcorn-chef-2.controls:
Pour kernels into the jar or
Back to menu

popcorn-time.title: Popcorn Time
popcorn-time.description:
Popcorn Time reduces snack preparation to a pure spatial reasoning challenge. A popcorn machine pops kernels one by one and you must release exactly the right quantity to fill the target container — judged entirely by eye, with no measuring tools and no second chances on each kernel batch.

The containers range from small paper cones to large cinema buckets, each requiring different estimates of volume and depth. Getting it exactly right is deeply satisfying; getting it very wrong is spectacular. A Goldilocks game where too little and too much are both failures, and just right is the only goal that matters.
intro: Test your eye for measurement: fill the container with exactly the right number of kernels.
The bucket size changes every round — spatial reasoning is your only guide.
popcorn-time.controls: Pop the popcorn or

real-snakesio.title: Real Snakes.io
real-snakesio.description:
Real Snakes.io throws you into a crowded multiplayer arena where dozens of other snakes are trying to do exactly what you're doing: collect glowing particles, grow as large as possible, and survive. The strategic twist is that you can cut across a rival's path — if they crash into your body, they explode into a pile of particles you can feast on.

Bigger snakes block more space and can trap smaller ones, but a nimble small snake can easily lure a massive one into a wall. Boost speed is available but shrinks your length — a calculated trade-off. Every session plays differently based on the other players in your arena.
intro: Battle hundreds of other snakes in a shared arena — eat particles, grow, and cut rivals off.
The longest snake in the arena is the most dangerous and the biggest target at once.
real-snakesio.controls:
Steer your snake
Burst of speed or

retro-basketball.title: Retro Basketball
retro-basketball.description:
Retro Basketball is as stripped-down as sports games get. A pixelated basketball court, a hoop, and a single mechanic: tap at the right moment to release the ball on a perfect arc. Score once and the hoop moves to a new distance and position. Score again and it moves again.

The challenge is pure timing — the release window is generous at first but the game has a way of making you suddenly forget where the sweet spot is when you're three baskets into a streak that you really don't want to break. Old-school charm wrapped around genuinely tense gameplay.
intro: Nail the perfect release with one tap in this retro pixel basketball arcade.
The hoop shifts position after every basket — timing and arc judgement are everything.
retro-basketball.controls: Launch the ball toward the hoop

slalom-canoe.title: Slalom Canoe
slalom-canoe.description:
Slalom canoe is one of the most technical sports in the Olympics: paddlers navigate a series of hanging gates on a churning white-water course, and the time penalties for missing or touching a gate can cost a medal. This Google Doodle recreates that experience with a tilting kayak that responds to your steering input against the current.

Green gates must be entered from the correct direction — hitting them backwards adds a penalty. Red gates must be avoided entirely. The river's current wants to push you off course constantly, so paddling against the flow is as important as paddling toward the gate. Satisfying, athletic, and more technical than it first appears.
intro: Race through Olympic slalom gates on a roaring mountain river — miss one and your time is ruined.
Green gates must be hit in the right direction; red ones must be avoided entirely.
slalom-canoe.controls: Paddle through the gates or or

slitherio.title: Slither Snake Online (Slither.io)
slitherio.description:
Slither.io is the multiplayer snake game that became a global phenomenon in 2016. Thousands of players share the same arena, each controlling a snake that grows by eating glowing particles. The twist that makes it brilliant: you can't attack other snakes directly, but if you cut across their path and they run into your body, they die — and their entire mass becomes particles you can absorb.

Small snakes are nimble; giant snakes are powerful but harder to steer. The constant risk-reward calculation of approaching a larger snake to steal their kill — or retreating to safety while you grow — creates compelling moment-to-moment tension. Just one more run. Every time.
intro: Grow your snake in a massive multiplayer arena by eating particles and outmanoeuvring rivals.
One crash ends everything — but making a bigger snake crash into you ends them.
slitherio.controls:
Steer the worm or or
Full-speed boost

snake.title: Google Snake Game
snake.description:
Google's Snake Doodle appeared on Easter Sunday 2013, reviving the game that Nokia had installed on millions of phones in the late 1990s and introduced an entire generation to mobile gaming. Navigate the snake around the board eating pieces of food — each bite makes it one segment longer.

The game is pure and unforgiving. Nothing punishes you except your own tail and the walls at the edge of the grid. As the snake grows the options narrow, the turns become more desperate, and the eventual collision feels both inevitable and surprising. A timeless design that holds up perfectly four decades after its invention.
intro: Guide a growing snake around a grid, eat every piece of food, and never cross your own tail.
Google's Easter 2013 Doodle — a perfect revival of the game that defined Nokia phones.
snake.controls:
Steer the snake or
Restart the round

snake-fit.title: Snake Fit
snake-fit.description:
