Snake Fit replaces the food-chasing mechanic with a pure spatial puzzle: your snake must stretch to cover every single tile on the grid, leaving no empty squares. The snake still grows as it moves, but here growth is the goal rather than a side effect. Every tile must be filled before the room is complete.

Planning the route is everything — attempt a path that dead-ends and there's no way to cover the remaining tiles. The grid gets larger and gains obstacles in later levels, demanding increasingly long chains of careful reasoning. An inspired reinvention of a 40-year-old formula.
intro: Fill every tile in the room with your snake's body — a clever puzzle twist on the classic.
One wrong move and the grid locks up. Think several steps ahead every time.
snake-fit.controls:
Guide the snake or
Switch character
Advance to the next level

snake-football.title: Snake Football
snake-football.description:
Snake Football answers the question nobody asked but everyone needed answered: what happens when you combine Snake with association football? Two snakes share a pitch with an oversized ball, each trying to manoeuvre their growing body into the ball and push it over the opponent's goal line.

Both snakes grow continuously, which means the open space on the pitch shrinks with every second. Early game is tactical; late game is pure chaos as two enormous serpents thrash around a nearly full pitch trying to score without accidentally gifting an own goal. First to three wins, winner earns the right to explain the sport to confused onlookers.
intro: Two snakes play football — grow your serpent, manoeuvre the ball, and score three goals.
Every second you survive makes the pitch more chaotic — and the match more hilarious.
snake-football.controls: Steer the snake

snake1.title: Snake
snake1.description:
Snake is one of the oldest video game concepts still in regular play. A single serpent on a bounded grid, food appearing one piece at a time, and the inescapable law that your body follows wherever your head leads. Eat an apple and grow one segment longer. Grow long enough and manoeuvring becomes a genuine puzzle.

There is no time limit, no enemy, no external threat — just you and the geometric logic of your own tail. The end, when it comes, is always your own fault and always feels absolutely fair. No game has a better ratio of rules-to-depth, and no game has wasted more productive hours worldwide. A masterpiece.
intro: Eat apples, grow longer, and never cross your own tail — the original Snake, unchanged.
Simple enough to learn in ten seconds. Impossible to truly master.
snake1.controls:
Player 1
Steer the snake (Player 1) or

soccer-2012.title: Soccer 2012
soccer-2012.description:
Soccer 2012 was Google's contribution to the Olympics excitement of London in July 2012. You play as the goalkeeper in a never-ending penalty shootout: a succession of animated kickers step up, wind back, and fire. Your job is to pick a direction and time your dive correctly.

The early rounds are gentle; the ball moves slowly enough to allow reading and reacting. As the shootout progresses the shots get harder, faster, and placed into corners that require perfect timing to reach. A lovely, simple game that delivers the exact tension of a penalty kick in 15 seconds of play.
intro: Dive left or right and save penalties in Google's London 2012 Olympics goalkeeper Doodle.
Later rounds add pace and placement — only the sharpest reflexes survive.
soccer-2012.controls:
Move the goalkeeper or
Dive / jump or or

solitaire.title: Google Solitaire
solitaire.description:
Google Solitaire is a polished implementation of Klondike — the definitive solitaire variant, and the one pre-installed on every Windows computer from 1990 onwards. Choose draw-one for a more forgiving game or draw-three for a proper challenge, then work through the deck building alternating-colour columns and ace-to-king foundation piles.

The rules are exactly as you remember. The interface is clean, fast, and intuitive. There's an undo button for the genuinely stuck, and a satisfying cascade animation when the final foundation is complete. Sometimes the classics are classics for very good reasons.
intro: Classic Klondike Solitaire in Google's clean, no-fuss browser implementation.
Draw one or three, build your foundations, and clear the deck with pure focus.
solitaire.controls: Drag cards to build stacks

space-invaders-google.title: Space Invaders Google
space-invaders-google.description:
Space Invaders invented the shoot-'em-up genre in 1978 and the formula has never been improved upon: rows of alien ships march back and forth across the screen, descending a step each time they reach the edge. Slide your cannon, shoot upward, and clear each row before the fleet reaches the bottom.

Crumbling bunkers provide temporary shelter from the alien's return fire. The fleet accelerates with every ship you destroy — by the time the last handful remain they're moving at terrifying speed, requiring rapid repositioning and pinpoint shooting. A perfect arcade game, free in your browser, exactly as it was designed nearly 50 years ago.
intro: Blast descending alien rows before they reach Earth — the arcade classic reimagined.
The fleet speeds up as it shrinks; the last alien is always the hardest.
space-invaders-google.controls:
Move the cannon
Fire
Pause
Quit
Lower resolution

stickman-hook.title: Stickman Hook
stickman-hook.description:
Stickman Hook is a pure momentum game. Your stickman hangs from a hook, swings in an arc, releases at the peak, and must catch the next hook before plummeting into the void. The controls are a single tap or click — press to attach, release to fly. That's it. The depth is entirely in the timing.

Release early and you don't carry enough momentum to reach the next hook. Release late and the angle is wrong. Release at exactly the right moment and you soar across the gap, converting kinetic energy into a perfect continuous swing. Mastering the rhythm produces a genuine feeling of grace and flow.
intro: Tap to grab the hook, release to swing — chain perfect arcs to fly through each level.
The rhythm is everything. Find it and the game feels like flying.
stickman-hook.controls: Swing on the bungee cord or

stickman-party.title: Stickman party
stickman-party.description:
Stickman Party collects eight wildly different mini-game challenges under one roof, each designed for two-player local competition. Race to the finish, dodge obstacles, hammer buttons faster, or outmanoeuvre your opponent in physics brawls — the format changes every round, keeping both players perpetually on edge.

The stickman art style strips everything down to pure readable action, which means split-second decisions are never obscured by visual noise. Win the most rounds across the bracket to claim the championship. The loser will insist the controller was broken. It wasn't.
intro: Battle through eight frantic mini-games to become the ultimate stickman party champion.
Win the most rounds — your rival will demand a rematch, which you should graciously accept.
stickman-party.controls:
Player 1
Control your stickman (Player 1)

swing-dancing-and-the-savoy-ballroom.title: Swing Dancing And The Savoy Ballroom!
swing-dancing-and-the-savoy-ballroom.description:
The Savoy Ballroom opened in Harlem in 1926 and for two decades it was the most important dance venue in America — the place where the Lindy Hop was born, where Ella Fitzgerald performed, and where Black and white dancers shared a floor at a time when that was genuinely radical. This Google Doodle celebrates the Savoy and the swing era with a rhythm game.

Tap, hold, and release in time with the music to keep your animated dancers moving. Build a combo by landing consecutive perfect beats, and watch the dance floor come alive around you. The music is joyful, the animation is charming, and the cultural history lesson is seamlessly embedded in every bar.
intro: Tap to the beat and swing dance through a Harlem ballroom in this rhythm Doodle.
The Savoy Ballroom was the birthplace of swing — feel the music and keep the combo alive.
swing-dancing-and-the-savoy-ballroom.controls:
Player 1
Catch the beat (Player 1) or or or
Pause

t-rex-run-3d-google.title: T-Rex Run 3D
t-rex-run-3d-google.description:
T-Rex Run 3D takes the classic Chrome offline game and rebuilds it entirely in three dimensions. The desert now stretches into the distance as the camera follows T-Rex from behind, turning the flat 2D obstacle course into a proper 3D runner. Cacti, pterodactyls, and new three-dimensional hazards approach from the edges of the screen as well as straight ahead.

The additional dimension initially disorients even veteran dino runners — depth perception adds a new judgement call to every jump. The acceleration curve mirrors the original but the increased visual information makes for a genuinely different experience. A worthy reinvention of an all-time classic.
intro: The beloved pixel dino gets a full 3D makeover — dodge obstacles coming from every angle.
The added depth means hazards can surprise you from the sides. Stay alert.
t-rex-run-3d-google.controls:
Jump over obstacles or
Duck under obstacles or

table-tennis-world-tour.title: Table Tennis World Tour
table-tennis-world-tour.description:
Table Tennis World Tour is a full-featured ping-pong game with a tournament career mode. Begin at regional club level, defeat the local champions, and earn ranking points to qualify for international competitions. Each opponent has a distinct playing style — some favour heavy topspin, others live for the backhand flick.

Your racket responds to timing and direction input: early contact produces a flat drive; late contact generates topspin that dips sharply off the bounce. Reading your opponent's shots, anticipating placement, and choosing the right moment to rip a winner down the line is the satisfying core of a genuinely deep table tennis simulation.
intro: Rally, read spins, and win tournaments against world-class ping-pong players worldwide.
Time your counter-attack, vary your placement, and break your opponent's rhythm to advance.
table-tennis-world-tour.controls: Swing your paddle

tap-tap-goal.title: Tap Tap Goal
tap-tap-goal.description:
Tap Tap Goal strips football down to a single mechanic: tap left to kick toward the left goal, tap right toward the right goal, and keep the ball in the air for as long as possible. There are no teammates, no goalkeeper, and no opposition — just you, the ball, and the metronome rhythm of your own kicking.

The satisfaction comes from building a long unbroken streak of clean contacts. Mistiming a tap brings the ball down to earth and the rally ends. It's football as pure reflex game — meditative at low scores, intensely focused at high ones. An ideal 90-second distraction that somehow always turns into 20 minutes.
intro: Tap left and right to swing your foot and score as many solo goals as possible.
Keep the rally going and rack up goals before you fluff a kick.
tap-tap-goal.controls: Tap to juggle the ball or

temple-of-boom.title: Temple Of Boom 🕹️ Game Temple of Boom free
temple-of-boom.description:
Temple of Boom is a two-player co-operative shooter set inside an ancient temple packed with skeletons, zombies, and armed bandits. Each room is a contained arena — clear every enemy to unlock the door to the next. Weapons scatter across the floors: pistols, shotguns, grenades, and more powerful options as you descend deeper.

Co-operation is key. One player can revive the other if they fall; covering different angles prevents being surrounded. The rooms escalate quickly in enemy count and aggression, forcing the two of you to communicate and coordinate even when chaos is breaking out on every screen edge. Loud, silly, and enormously fun.
intro: Blast through monster-filled temple rooms with a friend and an ever-growing arsenal.
Grab every weapon you can find and survive increasingly dangerous waves together.
temple-of-boom.controls:
Player 1
Jump / double-jump
Move
Pick up weapons
Shoot
Swap weapon
Menu

temple-run-2.title: Temple Run 2
temple-run-2.description:
Temple Run 2 is one of the defining endless runner games. You've stolen an ancient idol from its temple, the demonic guardian is right behind you, and the only option is to run. Swipe left or right to turn at corners, swipe up to jump over obstacles, and swipe down to slide under barriers as the path twists through jungle canopies, mine shafts, and clifftop ledges.

Coins collected during runs unlock upgrades that extend runs further: better starting speed, longer coin magnets, power-up head starts. Each run feels meaningfully different from the last, and chasing your personal distance record is an endlessly renewable motivation. A polished, satisfying game that perfected its genre.
intro: Swipe to run, turn, jump, and slide through the jungle while a demonic gorilla gains ground.
Collect coins for upgrades and push your personal distance record further with every run.
temple-run-2.controls:
Swipe to run and dodge or or
Power boost
Pause / Menu

the-mergest-kingdom.title: The Mergest Kingdom
the-mergest-kingdom.description:
The Mergest Kingdom is a merge puzzle game with light kingdom-building elements. Your once-great realm has been consumed by a spreading darkness, and the only way to push it back is to merge identical objects — two saplings become a young tree, two young trees become a full grove, two groves clear a section of corrupted land.

The chain goes deep: simple objects combine into complex ones that unlock new merge tiers, gradually restoring buildings, crops, and inhabitants to your kingdom. There's no time pressure — just the meditative satisfaction of finding matching pairs and watching the board transform. Surprisingly strategic and completely absorbing.
intro: Merge identical items to create higher-tier objects and rebuild a kingdom claimed by darkness.
Soothing, strategic, and deeply satisfying — the merge chain goes deeper than you expect.
the-mergest-kingdom.controls: Merge items to rebuild the kingdom or

the-scale-of-the-universe-online.title: The Scale of the Universe Online
the-scale-of-the-universe-online.description:
The Scale of the Universe is an interactive size comparison that places every object in the known universe on a single scrollable scale. Start at the quantum foam — structures smaller than a proton — and slide rightward through atoms, molecules, cells, insects, humans, cities, continents, planets, stars, nebulae, galaxies, and finally the full observable universe.

Each stop on the scale includes a measurement and a brief description. The cumulative effect of scrolling from one end to the other is genuinely disorienting in the best way — a visceral demonstration of how incomprehensibly large existence is, and how impossibly small we are within it. One of the most effective educational tools ever built for a browser.
intro: Zoom from a single neutrino all the way out to the observable universe in one smooth scroll.
A humbling, beautiful journey through 60 orders of magnitude.
the-scale-of-the-universe-online.controls:
Tap an object to learn about it
Scroll to zoom

tic-tac-toe.title: Google Tic-Tac-Toe
tic-tac-toe.description:
Tic-Tac-Toe is one of the oldest strategy games in human history — evidence of similar grid games has been found in ancient Egypt. The rules are universal: two players take turns marking a 3×3 grid, and the first to place three of their marks in a row — horizontal, vertical, or diagonal — wins.

Play against Google's AI at multiple difficulty levels, or sit across from a friend for a local game. At the easiest level, beginners can practise without frustration. At the hardest, the AI plays optimally — meaning every game ends in a draw unless you make a mistake. A perfect demonstration of why simple rules can produce deep strategic consequences.
intro: Classic Tic-Tac-Toe against a smart AI or a local friend — get three in a row to win.
Simple enough to teach a child. Surprisingly deep when played against a well-programmed opponent.
tic-tac-toe.controls:
Player 1
Place your mark (Player 1) or

toilet-rush.title: Toilet Rush
toilet-rush.description:
Toilet Rush is built on a premise of universal relatability: Trollface desperately needs to find a bathroom and the world has conspired to place as many obstacles as possible between him and it. Navigate through puzzles, crowds, locked doors, and genuinely baffling architectural choices under increasing time pressure.

Each level escalates the absurdity: the obstacles get weirder, the corridors get longer, and Trollface's expression becomes more urgently pained. The solution to each puzzle is usually obvious once you stop laughing at the situation long enough to think about it. Short, funny, and perfectly paced.
intro: Trollface urgently needs the bathroom — help him sprint through obstacles and reach it in time.
Navigate crowds, doors, and increasingly ridiculous barriers against the clock.
toilet-rush.controls:
Move Trollface or
Jump or

uno-online.title: Uno Multiplayer Online
uno-online.description:
UNO Online brings the classic card game to the browser with full multiplayer. Join a public table or create a private room for friends. The rules are exactly as you know them: match colour or number, play action cards to reverse direction, skip opponents, and force them to draw two or four. When one card remains, you must call UNO — forget, and get caught, and you draw four penalty cards.

Online play strips away the social negotiations of the physical game and replaces them with pure strategic card play. Hoard your action cards for the moment they'll cause maximum chaos, bluff your opponents about your hand size, and time the final card perfectly. A legitimate test of tactical thinking wrapped in a familiar family-game package.
intro: Play UNO against real opponents online — match colours, drop action cards, and shout Uno!
First player to empty their hand wins. Your rivals will do everything possible to prevent it.
uno-online.controls: Play a card from your hand or

zuma-deluxe.title: Zuma Deluxe
zuma-deluxe.description:
Zuma Deluxe is a timeless arcade puzzler. A chain of coloured balls winds along a curving track toward a pit at the end — let the chain reach the pit and your civilisation is destroyed. Your weapon is a stone frog that rotates to aim and launches balls from its mouth. Shoot a ball into the chain to create a group of three or more of the same colour, and that group explodes.

Combos cascade when an explosion brings two other same-coloured groups together, clearing large sections at once. Power-up balls appear in the chain — accuracy, explosive, and slow shots — that can turn the tide of a desperate situation. The chain never stops and the pressure never relents. A genuinely great puzzle game.
intro: Match coloured balls to destroy the chain before it rolls into the skull-shaped pit.
Aim your stone frog launcher, create groups of three, and beat back the endless advance.
zuma-deluxe.controls: Aim and fire the ball
